title: Stakeholder Outcomes, Owner Authorization, Ethics, and Evidence · Type-System Trace: Evidence Layers
summary: A request to audit a public website names pages to collect but no owner, user decision, permitted purpose, stop contact, privacy boundary, or proof that findings are useful. Predict authorization, URL identity, runtime admission, Promise ownership, resource bounds, accessibility impact, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate useful owner-authorized, privacy-minimized, inclusive crawl outcomes and claim layers; owner authorization and revocation must remain separate from robots and legal-review claims; then change one owner permission, seed, URL, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, media, or encoding decision and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate pure model, compiler, emitted Node, local HTTP, controlled site, browser, accessibility, security, load, and production claims.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Evidence Layers model · Theory Evidence Layers, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: One successful fetch proves the crawler and audit are production-ready.
